name: Freenove FNK0050 — measured (Marc, unit #01)
description: Measured drift profile from a real Freenove Robot Dog. Data from multiple hardware runs (2026-04-27 to 2026-05-05). Previous yaw_rate estimate of -0.4 was from LOST-phase only. Full phototaxis runs (2026-05-05) show 1.0-2.0 deg/s under walking load. The higher rate under load is caused by servo asymmetry amplified by CPG gait dynamics.

yaw_drift._history: -2.0 (v1, inflated by bias), -0.4 (v2, LOST phase only), -1.5 (v3, under walking load)

steering_effectiveness._comment: Measured: deg/s of yaw rotation per mm of CPG Z-offset (legacy, pre-PID)
steering_effectiveness.note: Z-offset steering replaced by asymmetric stride + PID in v0.5.0

notes: v3 profile (2026-05-05): drift rate updated from -0.4 to -1.5 based on phototaxis walking runs. Under walking load, servo asymmetry creates stronger drift than at rest. PID with Kp=0.05, Ki=0.01 compensates but needs ~30s for I-term to accumulate.
